message:
fcp-mesh/gossip: document MeshGossip::handle_summary as unauthenticated, pointing callers at MeshNode

`MeshGossip::handle_summary` is the lower-level state mutator that
takes a `GossipSummary` and advances the local peer map without
doing any signature verification. Since the recent commit added
signed-dispatch via `MeshNode::handle_summary`, it's easy for a
caller wiring up a new gossip path to grab `handle_summary` from
the gossip module and end up with a trust-all branch in production.

Add a rustdoc note on `MeshGossip::handle_summary` that:

* Explicitly calls out that callers on untrusted network paths
  MUST verify `summary.signature` before invoking this function.
* Points at `crate::node::MeshNode` as the authenticated dispatch
  entrypoint (i.e. the handler that does the verify-then-delegate
  pattern).

Docs-only change; no code or test changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
